behaviors.description: Evaluate-stage stores grounding in `grounding` artifact, not `plan`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module boundary (evaluate command -> artifact persistence key), not pure local logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: behavior checks destination slot, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: single stage write. Adversarial critique: this could be Unit if only a pure mapper function is under test; however the real regression is wrong persisted artifact key in wired command flow, so Unit can miss it. Rejection-gate: System/Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text only requires artifact-key routing in evaluate stage.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command in-process with a real artifact store implementation (local test store), then assert `grounding` exists/updated and `plan` is untouched by evaluate.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly requires command+store wiring; a lower Unit test would not catch miswired persistence.

behaviors.description: Implement-stage reads grounding when present and still writes execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires handoff between implement flow, artifact reader, and artifact writer. REAL-INFRA: no real external infra needed. LLM-DEP: this behavior is about read/write routing, not quality of generated content. MULTI-STEP: single implement stage with pre-existing grounding input. Adversarial critique: could be Agentic if correctness depended on model interpreting grounding semantically; disqualified by behavior text focusing on read-when-available and write-target invariants.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow with deterministic model stub, assert grounding was read/used flag path taken and output artifact written to `plan` while `grounding` remains intact.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assertions target cross-module artifact IO; unit-only branching test is insufficient.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive stops overwriting `plan` when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when deep-dive orchestration condition and artifact write side effect interact. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP: behavior is side-effect suppression given existing diagnosis, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: single stage side-effect check. Adversarial critique: could be Unit if condition is a pure predicate; but regression risk is accidental write in command wiring, which needs stage+store integration.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with artifact store attached, assert no `plan` write occurs (or existing `plan` unchanged) and other expected outputs remain.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs integration to catch unintended persistence side effect.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read earlier `grounding` in same run context.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is state handoff across modules (lineage/run-context identity + artifact lookup). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced fully in-process. LLM-DEP: behavior text is lineage/readability, not judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: two-stage handoff but not full agentic pipeline quality check. Adversarial critique: could be Workflow if multiple real model decisions are required to preserve handoff; disqualified because behavior text centers on run-context artifact lineage mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate (or directly write grounding) and then implement within same synthetic run context IDs using real lineage/store components; assert implement resolves the exact prior grounding artifact by lineage metadata.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower Unit tests cannot validate cross-component lineage propagation.

behaviors.description: In full `deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement` run, final implementation plan reflects earlier grounding.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure emerges across chained stages, not one boundary. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory OS/network, but pipeline execution is required. LLM-DEP: reflection of earlier grounding in final plan depends on model behavior through multiple stages. MULTI-STEP: explicitly multiple agentic steps in sequence, matching Workflow definition. Adversarial critique: could be Agentic if reduced to single model call; disqualified by behavior text explicitly requiring end-to-end multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real multi-stage agent pipeline end-to-end with representative issue input; assert produced final `plan` includes/uses grounding facts introduced earlier (with tolerant semantic checks across nondeterministic outputs).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A lower-level Agentic or Integration test would miss inter-step pipeline regressions.

behaviors.description: If grounding is absent, implement safely falls back, emits valid `plan`, and does not corrupt other artifacts.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires implement fallback path plus artifact-store mutation safety across keys. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: correctness is fallback/control-flow + artifact integrity, not output quality. MULTI-STEP: single implement execution with missing optional input. Adversarial critique: could be Unit if fallback is pure function; disqualified because corruption checks require real store interactions across multiple artifacts.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement in a context without `grounding` but with other preexisting artifacts; assert a valid `plan` is produced, no exceptions, and non-target artifacts are unchanged.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is required to observe unintended artifact mutations.
